risk_flags: Storage at 38.5% (15th-percentile, 14 pp below seasonal)—refill pace will anchor H2 generation mix and EUA curve.
GB Power 131.09 EUR/MWh (94th-percentile) signals transmission constraint or demand spike; monitor GB-DE spread widening.
Middle East conflict and Hormuz crisis elevate LNG supply risk; TTF repricing window on geopolitical premium still open.
Record May temperatures boost cooling demand but may impair southern EU hydro; net DA effect asymmetric by basin.

base.summary: Geopolitical premium holds; storage refill continues; heat-driven demand offsets mild renewable gains.

downside.summary: Hormuz de-escalation or Middle East ceasefire signals; US LNG exports surge; mild weather suppresses cooling demand; storage recovery accelerates.

upside.summary: Hormuz escalation or Suez closure disrupts LNG supply; EU growth cuts trigger fiscal stimulus; storage injection pace falls short.

themes: Geopolitical premium reasserts across power curve
Storage deficit elongates thermal risk into summer
GB power outlier; Clean Spark at 90th-percentile
Heat dome pressure on hydro; US LNG capacity upside via TTF arb
top_drivers: Middle East conflict and Hormuz supply risk elevate geopolitical premium across EU power and gas; TTF curve repricing window open.
Storage at 15th-percentile (38.5%, 14 pp below seasonal) locks in H2 thermal call and anchors EUA dispatch margin risk.
GB Power at 94th-percentile (131.09 EUR/MWh) signals transmission constraint or demand spike; Clean Spark at 90th-percentile confirms gas-to-coal advantage collapse.
Record May heat boosts near-term cooling demand but risks southern EU hydro output; net DA effect data-dependent by basin.
top_takeaway: GB Power at 94th-percentile amid Middle East geopolitical premium and record May heat; storage 14 pp below seasonal—refill pace critical for H2 thermal call.

watchlist.why: At 38.5% and 14 pp below seasonal, summer maintenance and H2 thermal adequacy hinge on June-August injection trajectory.
GB at 94th-percentile (131.09 EUR/MWh) vs DE at 76th-percentile (140.12 EUR/MWh)—inverse signal suggests transmission or supply asymmetry; watch daily arbitrage window.
NATO-led security discussions late May 2026 will reset LNG supply premium; watch TTF curve flattening or re-steepening on escalation/de-escalation messaging.
44.9 Bcf/d pipeline capacity additions (70% from Texas) will lift US export optionality and create TTF downside arbitrage if EU storage recovers.
Structural US power demand growth may crowd out LNG export capacity or raise US gen costs, indirectly supporting TTF and EU power prices via tighter global LNG.

watchlist_dated.name: NATO Strait of Hormuz security discussion
watchlist_dated.why: Geopolitical risk reset on LNG/crude supply; TTF curve repricing window.

watchlist_dated.name: US pipeline capacity commissioning (44.9 Bcf/d)
watchlist_dated.why: LNG export growth may lower TTF arbitrage and reduce EU supply scarcity premium.
